title: MANGA Is there a way
wikitext:
[]: Is there a way             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Mon Nov 19, 2007 3:18 pm Is there a way a person could read the manga without relying on translations? (i'm thinking of something along the lines of a Japanese to english translator) Just wondering.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Nov 19, 2007 3:40 pm learn Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Mon Nov 19, 2007 3:46 pm oh, i'm currently trying to do so.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Nov 19, 2007 3:58 pm Look at the pictures and just make up the words. You'd have your very own Evangelion: Choose Your Own Adventure story right there.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Nov 19, 2007 11:44 pm OH THE POSSIBILITIES!! I could name Shinji "Don McWunderwubble", and his prime motivation in life could be fighting for the sake of rescuing Princess Yuki from Emperor Hairy Michel Foucalt. Kaji would be the result of James Bond/Han Solo/Ash gene splicing. Asuka would have a thick Russian accent, and her first line would be "It was I killed the old pawnbroker woman and her sister Lizaveta with an axe and robbed them." Fuyutsuki would be a really ugly woman. All of Misato's flashback lines would simply consist of descriptions of freak accidents from her days as a clown.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 20, 2007 12:39 am Damn Brik, when did you become so fucking hilarious?

[]:             Quote Post by Darknemo2000 » Tue Nov 20, 2007 2:58 pm As far as machine translator goes... All of them are much worser tha human translator specially online ones. I would recommend you trying ATLAS V 12 for now. Yet for it to be able to translate you would need an Japanese character recognition tool that would be able to recognize Japanese text in pictures and to put it in text that you can copy/paste into ATLAS later. Sadly all of optical character recognition tools are rather crappy. The best I know for the date is readiris but for it to work properly you would nbeed a REALLY REALLY clean manga scan that would use proper kanji or harigana script as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Tue Nov 20, 2007 3:46 pm Hmm . . . learn Japanese. Or just gather from what they say from the pictures.
